title: Wrapper objects should be avoided for primitive types
description: Wrapper objects should not be used for primitive typesAnalyze your codeconsistency-conventionalmaintainabilityCode SmellQuick FixIDE quick fixes available with SonarQube for IDEpitfallWhy is this an issue?More InfoThe JavaScript wrapper objectsNumber,String, andBooleanprovide a way to work with their respective primitive types (number,stringandboolean) as objects.Using wrapper can lead to unexpected behavior due to the differences in how they are compared and used in operations compared to primitive types. It can also lead to unnecessary memory allocation and slower code execution.let x = new Number("0"); // Noncompliant: x is an object, not a primitive if (x) { alert('hi'); // Shows 'hi'. }Remove thenewkeyword to get the primitive value instead of a wrapper object.let x = Number("0"); if
recommendation: Remove the 'new' keyword when using Number(), String(), or Boolean() to get primitive values instead of wrapper objects.

checks.message: Wrapper objects should not be used for primitive types - use primitive values directly
